mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, it signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized consumer demand recovery that boosts corporate revenues and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate output and positive forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in >50% of observations during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to generate positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that directly supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals an immediate supply-side contraction that triggers risk-off positioning and negative forward equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks fail to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
